\doxysubsection{Detailed Description}
Streaming, stack-\/based reader for simple tag-\/delimited job files. 

The parser operates directly on an std\+::ifstream. It tracks the current open tag using an internal stack ( {\ttfamily tags} ). Opening tags push onto the stack; closing tags pop it. The API exposes methods to open/close a file, read the next tag token, query the current tag, read a typed value, and skip to the end of the current tag block.

\begin{DoxyWarning}{Warning}
This is {\bfseries{not}} a full XML parser. It expects tokens separated by whitespace, with tags like {\ttfamily \texorpdfstring{$<$}{<}name\texorpdfstring{$>$}{>}} and {\ttfamily \texorpdfstring{$<$}{<}/name\texorpdfstring{$>$}{>}} appearing as single tokens. Attributes are not supported. 
\end{DoxyWarning}

int Job\+Parser\+::end\+Of\+Tag (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}{}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Skip tokens until the matching closing tag of the current block. 

Consume tokens until the closing tag of the current block is found.

Reads tokens using the stream extraction operator until {\ttfamily \texorpdfstring{$<$}{<}/get\+Tag()\texorpdfstring{$>$}{>}} is encountered, then returns. Intended to quickly skip over blocks you do not wish to parse. \begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
0 on completion.
\end{DoxyReturn}
Builds the token {\ttfamily \texorpdfstring{$<$}{<}/get\+Tag()\texorpdfstring{$>$}{>}} and keeps reading whitespace-\/delimited tokens until an exact match is encountered. \begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
0 when the closing tag token has been read (or EOF reached). 
\end{DoxyReturn}

template$<$class Type$>$ \\
Type Job\+Parser\+::read (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}{}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Read a value of type {\ttfamily Type} from the stream using operator\texorpdfstring{$>$}{>}\texorpdfstring{$>$}{>}.

{\em Type} & to read. \\

\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
The value read.
\end{DoxyReturn}
Consumes the next whitespace-\/delimited token from the underlying stream and converts it to {\ttfamily Type}. No comment handling or tag awareness is performed here; prefer to call this after \doxylink{classJobParser_a83fe4cb0b8be6a82f39379fb516341ae}{read\+Tag} has positioned you inside the right block. 

Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{JobParser_8h_source_l00153}{153}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{JobParser_8h_source}{Job\+Parser.\+h}}.

\Hypertarget{classJobParser_a83fe4cb0b8be6a82f39379fb516341ae}\index{JobParser@{JobParser}!readTag@{readTag}}
\index{readTag@{readTag}!JobParser@{JobParser}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{readTag()}{readTag()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{classJobParser_a83fe4cb0b8be6a82f39379fb516341ae} 
std\+::string Job\+Parser\+::read\+Tag (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}{}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Read the next tag token and update the open-\/tag stack. 

Read the next tag (opening or closing) and update the tag stack.

If the next non-\/whitespace character is \textquotesingle{}\texorpdfstring{$<$}{<}\textquotesingle{}, the token following it is interpreted as a tag (with the trailing \textquotesingle{}\texorpdfstring{$>$}{>}\textquotesingle{} stripped). Opening tags are pushed to the stack; closing tags (starting with \textquotesingle{}/\textquotesingle{}) pop if they match the current top.

\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
The name of the current open tag after the update, or an empty string if there are no open tags.
\end{DoxyReturn}
Inspects the next non-\/whitespace character; if it is \textquotesingle{}\texorpdfstring{$<$}{<}\textquotesingle{}, extracts the tag token (stripping the trailing \textquotesingle{}\texorpdfstring{$>$}{>}\textquotesingle{}). If the tag does not start with \textquotesingle{}/\textquotesingle{}, it is pushed onto the tag stack. Otherwise, the stack is popped if the closing tag matches the current top; if it does not, a warning is printed.

\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
The name of the currently open tag (stack top) after processing, or an empty string if the stack becomes empty. 
\end{DoxyReturn}
